Paranormal Activity: The Marked Ones (2014)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $32,462,372Details
International Box Office $58,442,482
Worldwide Box Office $90,904,854
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $1,539,046
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,026,547
Total $2,565,593
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Recent high school grad Jesse—who has just bought his first hand-held camera, go figure—spends the summer hanging out with best pals Hector and Marisol. All is not well, however; the creepy lady downstairs, considered by many to be a bruja, is murdered by class valedictorian Oscar not long after Jesse lowers a camera through the vent and catches his neighbor painting a strange symbol on a naked woman’s stomach.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$18,343,611 (56.5% of total gross)
Legs:1.77 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:35.7% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$5,000,000 (worldwide box office is 18.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,867 opening theaters/2,883 max. theaters, 2.8 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $73,040,016

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: January 3rd, 2014 (Wide) by Paramount Pictures
International Releases: January 23rd, 2014 (Limited) (Australia)
Video Release: April 8th, 2014 by Paramount Home Video
MPA Rating: R for pervasive language, some violence, graphic nudity and some drug use.
(Rating bulletin 2302, 12/18/2013)
Running Time: 84 minutes
Franchise: Paranormal Activity
Cast, crew, or production detail: Filmed By a Character, Found Footage, Intertitle, Prologue
Plot point: Occult, Possessed
Social setting: Supernatural
Subgenre: Supernatural Horror
Target audience: Hispanic
Source:Spin-Off
Genre:Horror
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Blumhouse, Solana Films
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English, Spanish

Recent high school grad Jesse—who has just bought his first hand-held camera, go figure—spends the summer hanging out with best pals Hector and Marisol. All is not well, however; the creepy lady downstairs, considered by many to be a bruja, is murdered by class valedictorian Oscar not long after Jesse lowers a camera through the vent and catches his neighbor painting a strange symbol on a naked woman’s stomach.
